nameMeanings.name: Somes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Saham Toney (Norfolk), Earl and Monk Soham (Suffolk), or Soham (Cambridgeshire), and perhaps also Siam Hall near Newton (Suffolk), all of which share the same etymology (‘lake estate’, Old English sǣ ‘sea, lake’ + hām ‘village, homestead, estate’).

variants.name: Sones
meanings: perhaps an altered form of Soames; see Somes .

variant of Soane, with post-medieval excrescent -s (see Son ). The -o- in Middle English and early modern English son(e) is a conventional spelling of /u/ before an n. This /u/ became /ʌ/ in Standard English, hence the current pronunciation of Son. If /u/ was lengthened it became /o:/ in Middle English, and then /u:/ in modern English, hence the occasional spelling Soon. In some speakers' dialects, however, it seems that Middle English /u/ may have been lowered to /ɔ/ and lengthened to /ɔ:/, becoming modern English /əu/ represented in the spelling Soane. At any rate, Soon and Soane (also Sone) appear together in the same counties and are presumably alternative pronunciations of the same name.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Somes was born about 1580, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. She married William Somes about 1599, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Somes est née environ 1580, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé William Somes environ 1599, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elleenterrée à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Somes wurde um 1580 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1599 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom William Somes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Somes è nata il circa 1580 nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato William Somes circa 1599, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Fu sepolta a England.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. William Somes(은)는 대략 1580년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1599년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 William Somes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국에 묻혔다.
